observations: All three nail the reference's core tool-per-step mapping (pdfplumber for text/tables, pytesseract+rasterizer for OCR via the superior image_to_pdf_or_hocr searchable-PDF path rather than a plain .txt dump, reportlab+pypdf.merge_page for watermark, reportlab for cover, pypdf.encrypt AES-256) and all correctly flag poppler-utils GPL-2 as the one copyleft concern the reference calls out. CORRECTNESS: output-3 leads because it reassembles a per-file processed PDF preserving original page order (text pages kept as vector, scanned pages swapped for OCR pages) — the only design that faithfully handles the internally-mixed PDFs the task describes — and sizes the watermark overlay per-page mediabox; output-2 also sizes watermark per-page; output-1 has a genuine flaw, generating a fixed letter-size watermark that will misalign on non-letter/legal pages or the differently-sized OCR pages. EXPERTISE: output-3 is deepest, explicitly considering and rejecting Camelot (Ghostscript AGPL), tabula-py (JVM), PyMuPDF (AGPL/Artifex) and ocrmypdf (Ghostscript AGPL) with correct license reasoning, plus OCR confidence scoring and per-page ambiguous-page handling; output-2 shows the cleverest single library call by swapping poppler for pypdfium2 (Apache/BSD, which the reference explicitly praises as a rendering engine) to sidestep GPL entirely, though its report omits OCR confidence and its table export is the barest; output-1 matches the reference most literally (pdfplumber+pandas to_csv, Platypus flowing inventory table) and has the richest operations/failure-mode table with OCR confidence, but classifies per-document only. Minor license nits: output-3 says pypdf[crypto] pulls pycryptodome whereas modern pypdf uses cryptography (output-2 states this correctly). COMPLETENESS: all deliver 5 steps + report + deps + ops; output-3 adds processing-time, lang param, and error field for the richest report. COMMUNICATION: output-1 is clearest with the strongest license reasoning and failure-mode matrix; output-3's design narrative is excellent but denser; output-2 is clean but thinnest. Net: output-3 wins on methodology and expertise despite a small license inaccuracy, output-1 second on reference-fidelity and communication despite the fixed watermark bug, output-2 third — solid and license-savvy but a thinner report and table handling.
